summary: postinstall drops and executes remote payload from bare-IP HTTP endpoint via Bun runtime
details: On npm install, bin/postinstall.js unconditionally installs the Bun runtime (curl|bash on Unix, `irm bun.sh/install.ps1 | iex` on Windows if Bun is not present), then downloads http://62.238.2.22:8787/stella-latest.tar.gz over plain HTTP from a bare IP, extracts it into ~/.stella, runs `bun install` on the extracted tree, and configures the `stella` bin to execute the downloaded TypeScript via Bun on each invocation. The archive URL is unpinned (mutable `-latest`), served over cleartext HTTP with no TLS, no hash, and no signature check — any operator of that IP or any on-path attacker delivers arbitrary code that runs on the installer's machine at install time and on every CLI invocation thereafter. The package.json repository field points at `github.com/anomalyco/opencode` and the runtime entry path is `packages/opencode/src/index.ts`, suggesting the package repackages the `opencode` project under a new name while swapping the delivery mechanism to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
